steps.title: Identify Text 1's claim
steps.content: The underlined claim in Text 1 says The Nutcracker is stuck in the past and should no longer be produced. That is a complete rejection of the show, not just a call to change old-fashioned parts of it.

steps.title: Compare Text 2's position
steps.content: Text 2 partly agrees that the show is outdated, but it does not want companies to abandon it. Instead, Text 2 says the ballet remains a holiday favorite, provides important income for some companies, and can be updated creatively, as in Hot Chocolate Nutcracker.

steps.title: Choose the response
steps.content:
• Choice A: Incorrectly says Text 2 would reject updated versions, even though Text 2 praises one.
  • Choice B: Incorrectly says audiences have largely stopped attending, while Text 2 calls the show a holiday favorite.
  • Choice C: Overstates the income point by claiming most companies could increase income through modernized versions.
  • Choice D: Fits Text 2's view that companies should revise the show instead of completely rejecting it.
The answer is D.
